title: Bring-up re-run safety — idempotency + first-run race. A re-run of migrate / snapshot-replay / seed is either safe-and-idempotent (a 2nd run converges to the same state) or fails loudly with a guard — never silently doubles data, never aborts mid-surface. Owns the set -e first-run-race audit across the bring-up scripts (incl. the live docker-harness migrate-race behavior test inherited from M16 as Fate-2), the stacksnap replay re-run protection (per-stack-isolated TRUNCATE-then-reload), the stackseed re-run protection (idempotent COPY + casbin WHERE-NOT-EXISTS + the fixed --reset fleet), and the net-new corpus/ops/idempotency.md contract.
position_note: M17 is the 2nd of v1.3b 'dress rehearsal' (M16->M20). It hardens the bring-up PRIMITIVES (re-run safety) so the later milestones' auto-chaining (M20) is safe to retry. Depends on M16's clean pushed baseline.
kind: a code milestone (re-run guards + the first-run-race sweep) + one net-new corpus doc. The testable logic lives in the SEPARATE nested .agentspace/rosetta-extensions repo (Go: stack-seeding idempotent COPY + casbin guard + --reset fleet, stack-snapshot replay TRUNCATE-reload + target-class fence; Python: demo-stack set-e race fences + the live docker harness; shell: the 4 bring-up scripts). The rosetta m17/rerun-safety branch carries only corpus/ops/idempotency.md (net-new) + 5 wired-parent cross-links + the milestone tracking docs.

tests.language: go (the re-run guards — stack-seeding + stack-snapshot) + python (demo-stack set-e fences + the live docker migrate-race harness) + shellcheck/py_compile on the touched scripts + markdown (the net-new idempotency.md = the rosetta deliverable)
tests.go_note: +23 Go test funcs in M17. stack-seeding 236->252 (+16): pg/pg_test.go — the CopyRowsIdempotent temp-merge SQL builders (createTempLikeSQL, mergeFromTempSQL ON CONFLICT shape, single-column merge edge, the injection sweep over schema/table/tmp/cols/conflictCol), the validation/conflict-check error paths; seeders/seeders_test.go — the casbin g2 dedup predicate must compare ALL 7 columns (p_type+v0..v5); cmd/stackseed/main_test.go — every resetTables entry is exactly schema.table + no-duplicates (the destructive --reset surface). stack-snapshot 224->231 (+7): replay/replay_test.go — the ClearForReplay child-first clear + replace-not-append e2e; cmd/stacksnap/adapters_test.go — truncateForReplaySQL is ALWAYS a single-table TRUNCATE RESTART IDENTITY, never DROP/DELETE/CASCADE/cross-schema, degenerate identifiers stay a single-relation TRUNCATE, injection-quoted; reference/reference_test.go — the replay-twice-same-counts proof.
tests.python_note: +9 demo-stack (21->30 collected). test_tooling.py 21->27: TestSetEraceGuards — the set -e first-run-race fences (up-injected.sh GH_PAT loud-guard, the rosetta-demo/dev-stack DEV_PROJECT || true fallback, the migrate-demo.sh wait_pg/wait_sentinel_running defense-in-depth, the schema-create || log latent-site fix) can't regress. test_migrate_race_live.py (net-new, 3 tests): the LIVE docker harness (M16 Fate-2) runs the real migrate-demo.sh against a throwaway pgvector Postgres container IN the ISSUE-7 race state and asserts the script survives + seeds the policy + re-runs idempotently; skips cleanly without Docker (so the unit count stays static at 27). Python counts are pytest-collected (parametrized cases count individually); dev-stack = 33 test funcs / 38 collected.
tests.go_race: all 4 Go modules pass -race -count=1; gofmt + go vet clean.
tests.shellcheck: the 4 M17-touched shell scripts (migrate-demo.sh, up-injected.sh, rosetta-demo, dev-stack/dev-stack) shellcheck-CLEAN.
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean, sequential, on all 3 milestone-touched suites: stack-seeding (-race -shuffle), stack-snapshot (-race -shuffle), demo-stack (pytest, default order — pytest-randomly absent, matching M16). 0 flakes.

coverage.note: Harden ran 2 passes (recorded in progress.md). The pure SQL builders were already 100% covered; the harden deepened ENFORCEMENT (mutation-pins) on already-covered lines rather than chasing new statements.
coverage.stack-seeding_pg: 52.0% -> 55.3% statements (+3.3 — the CopyRowsIdempotent validation/conflict-check branch now exercised hermetically). Remaining uncovered = DB-touching (Connect/Exec/CopyIn/live merge), proven by the live docker harness.
coverage.stack-snapshot_replay: 100% (unchanged); cmd/stacksnap 77.0% (unchanged — remainder is live-DB adapter wiring).
coverage.cmd_stackseed: 47.0% (unchanged — the --reset pins are over the package-level resetTables const, zero executable statements; the rest is live-DB code covered by the docker harness).
coverage.mutation_proven: every M17 guard is mutation-pinned: removing the replay clear, the casbin WHERE-NOT-EXISTS, or the idempotent-COPY ON CONFLICT each fails its test, showing the exact doubling/abort. The truncateForReplaySQL target-class test fails if the SQL ever becomes a DROP/DELETE/CASCADE/cross-schema op.

prod_safety_verification.verdict: GREEN — the new TRUNCATE/idempotent-write logic is provably confined to per-stack-isolated offset targets.
prod_safety_verification.truncate_target_class: truncateForReplaySQL pinned by 4 tests: ExactShape (single-table TRUNCATE RESTART IDENTITY), IsAlwaysTruncateNeverDropOrDelete (never DROP/DELETE/CASCADE/cross-schema), DegenerateIdentifiers (degenerate/empty input stays a single-relation TRUNCATE, never a destructive class), QuotesAgainstInjection (QuoteIdent-escaped). The connection it runs on is pg.DSNForOffset(baseDSN, n) — the per-stack offset — so the TRUNCATE can structurally only ever land on the per-stack-isolated Postgres, never prod, never a shared store.
prod_safety_verification.reset_destructive_surface: doReset's TRUNCATE … CASCADE runs only over the resetTables package const (pinned: every entry exactly schema.table, no duplicates); the casbin g2 grant is NOT truncated (it shares the sentinel schema with init_policy.sql's global policy) — a targeted DELETE … WHERE p_type='g2' (resetCasbin) preserves bootstrap.
prod_safety_verification.m15_drift_guards: all GREEN after the safety.md 'See also' addition (the idempotency.md cross-link): read-side stack-seeding/isolation/safety_doc_drift_test.go (TestSafetyDocClerkHostsMatchCode, …DirectusTokenKeys, …ForcedBucketOverride, …NamesRealGuardSymbols) + write-side stack-snapshot/cmd/stacksnap/main_drift_test.go (TestSafetyDocPredicatesMatchCode, …NamesRealFirewallGates, …BoundedReadSessionMatchesCode). No way the new logic could TRUNCATE a non-per-stack target — no blocker.

delivered.idempotency_md: corpus/ops/idempotency.md (net-new, ~150 lines, dual-level + code-cited): the per-component re-run verdict table (migrate SAFE / replay SAFE-with-the-TRUNCATE-guard / seed SAFE-with-the-idempotent-guards), the two re-run models (re-run-in-place vs teardown-then-redo), and the engineer detail per guard. Wired bidirectionally into demo/README.md, root CLAUDE.md, snapshot-spec.md, seeding-spec.md, safety.md.
delivered.replay_guard: stack-snapshot/replay/replay.go: Replayer.ClearForReplay — per-stack-isolated TRUNCATE-then-reload, child-first (reverse dependency order, no CASCADE), before the parent-first COPY. A 2nd replay REPLACES, never appends. Safe-by-default, no flag (#M17-D3). The SQL is the pure truncateForReplaySQL (adapters.go), target-class pinned (#M17-D4).
delivered.seed_guards: stack-seeding: Conn.CopyRowsIdempotent (pg/pg.go) — COPY-to-session-local-TEMP-then-INSERT…SELECT…ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING in one tx, preserving the bulk path; all 7 seeders rewired (#M17-D5). The casbin g2 grant uses INSERT…SELECT…WHERE NOT EXISTS (the casbin tables have no unique tuple constraint) (#M17-D6). --reset extended to the full FK-ordered fleet (activity_events->jobsim sessions->skill_path_sessions->assignments->memberships->users->organizations) + a targeted casbin g2 DELETE, not TRUNCATE (#M17-D7).
delivered.race_audit: the set -e first-run-race audit across the 4 bring-up scripts: up-injected.sh GH_PAT fails loud (#M17-D1); rosetta-demo + dev-stack DEV_PROJECT || true so the documented fallback runs (#M17-D1); migrate-demo.sh wait_pg + wait_sentinel_running defense-in-depth, both bounded + non-fatal (#M17-D2); the schema-create || log latent-site fix surfaced by the live harness (#M17-D9).
delivered.live_harness: demo-stack/tests/test_migrate_race_live.py — the M16 Fate-2 item LANDED (#M17-D8): the real migrate-demo.sh run against a throwaway pgvector container in the race state, proving survival + idempotency. Skips without Docker.

review.findings_note: 1 finding — the decision-triage review surfaced that idempotency.md blends the D1-D7 mechanism rationale but lacked the (#M17-DK) back-ref tags the v1.3 corpus precedent uses (M10-Dx/M13-Dx). Added the tags so each mechanism (replay TRUNCATE-reload, idempotent COPY, casbin guard, --reset, the race sweep) traces to its decision record. No code/test/scope finding (the build+harden logic held under every probe).

notes: M17 is the 2nd milestone of v1.3b 'dress rehearsal' — it makes the bring-up PRIMITIVES re-run-safe so M20's auto-chaining is safe to retry. The defining risk was data-safety: the new TRUNCATE/idempotent-write logic must only ever hit a per-stack-isolated offset target. The close verified this holds (the truncateForReplaySQL target-class is pinned to a single-table TRUNCATE RESTART IDENTITY, never DROP/DELETE/CASCADE/cross-schema, and the connection is structurally the per-stack offset DSN; the M15 read-side+write-side safety drift guards stay GREEN; the --reset destructive surface is pinned to schema.table-only entries and the casbin policy is preserved via a targeted g2 DELETE). The close also ABSORBED M16's one Fate-2 item (the live docker migrate-race behavior test landed as M17-D8) and reconciled the dress-rehearsal-m17 tag from the build HEAD dcef026 to the final HEAD 0d36251 (the tag trailed the 2 harden commits), re-consuming stack-demo to it. Review found 1 docs Fate-1 (the decision ref-tags). Deferral re-audit GREEN. Go 713->736 (+23), Python 182->191 collected (+9), flake 0 (5/5 all 3 touched suites). Next: M18 (the verification safety net).
